did.unittitle: FULL TEST VERSION Alice and Hans Tischler Collection of Israeli and Jewish Composers

descriptivenote.p: English , Hebrew , Ladino , Yiddish , Spanish; Castilian , French , German , Latin , Italian , Russian , Georgian , Greek, Modern (1453-) , Japanese , Armenian .

did.abstract: The Alice and Hans Tischler Collection of Israeli and Jewish Composers, 1929-2008, consists of printed and recorded music written by Jewish and Israeli composers and collected by music librarian Alice Tischler and musicology professor Hans Tischler as well as the Tischers' research notes and other personal effects.
did.physloc: Cook Music Library (200 S Eagleson Ave, Bloomington, Indiana), Vault and Stacks

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Retrieval requires advance notice. Please contact the Cook Music Library to access this collection.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The donor(s) of this collection have not transferred their copyrights for the materials to the William & Gayle Cook Music Library. For more information, please contact the Music Library staff.

The William & Gayle Cook Music Library respects the intellectual property rights of others and does not claim any copyrights for non-university records, materials in the public domain, or materials for which we do not hold a Deed of Gift. Responsibility for the determination of the copyright status of these materials rests with those persons wishing to reuse the materials. Researchers are responsible for securing permission from copyright owners and any other rights holders for any reuse of these materials that extends beyond fair use or other statutory limitations.

Digital reproductions of archival materials from the William & Gayle Cook Music Library are made available for noncommercial educational and research purposes only. If you are the copyright holder for any of the digitized materials and have questions about its inclusion on our site, please contact the Cook Music Library staff for more details.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Alice and Hans Tischler, circa 1980-2010.

bioghist.head: Biographical Note
bioghist.p: Hans Tischler was born in Vienna, Austria, in 1915. While he completed his conducting and composition studies at the Vienna State Music Academy, he also studied piano with Paul Wittgenstein. In 1937, he earned a PhD in musicology from the University of Vienna with a dissertation on harmony in the music of Gustav Mahler. A year later, Tischler fled Vienna to escape the Nazi takeover of Austria, settling in the United States. There he completed a second PhD in musicology at Yale University, writing a dissertation on The Motet in 13th-Century France that marked the beginning of a distinguished career researching and editing medieval music. He obtained American citizenship after serving in the US Army from 1943 to 1945.

Hans was professor and music department chair at West Virginia Wesleyan College from 1945 to 1947, then moved to Roosevelt University in Chicago, spending one year as guest lecturer at the University of Chicago (1956-1957). He founded the Chicago chapter of the International Society for Contemporary Music in 1950 and served as its director for fifteen years. After his first wife passed away, Hans married Alice in 1958. In 1965, he joined the Musicology Department at Indiana University, where he taught for two decades, retiring in 1985. He was also a guest lecturer at Tel-Aviv University in 1972 and at Bar Ilan University in 1986.

Hans made his greatest contributions as a scholar and editor of medieval music, writing The Style and Evolution of the Earliest Motets (1985), publishing more than sixty articles in the field, and creating editions that include The Montpellier Codex (4 volumes, 1978-1985), Chanter m'estuet: Songs of the Trouvères (with Samuel N. Rosenberg, 1981), The Earliest Motets (to c. 1270) (3 volumes, 1982), The Parisian Two-Part Organa (1988), Trouvère Lyrics with Melodies (15 volumes, 1997), The Songs of the Master Trouvère Gace Brulé (2001), Conductus and Contrafacta (2001), The Earliest Laude (2002), and The Earliest Polyphonic Art Music (2005). He also published over twenty articles on Mozart, Mahler, Hindemith, music of the nineteenth and twentieth centuries, and aesthetics and pedagogy of music, as well as textbooks on music appreciation (The Perceptive Music Listener, 1955) and harmony (Practical Harmony, 1964). Hans's productivity was astonishing, with many of his most important contributions appearing in the two decades after his retirement from teaching. His final publication was a book chapter in 2007, seventy years after his dissertation, making his one of the longest careers in his field.

Hans's research and publications were funded by a Guggenheim fellowship, National Endowment for the Arts grants, and numerous other awards. He was a member of the American Musicological Society for over sixty years, giving his last presentation at the annual conference in 2009 when he was 94. He was also a member of the International Musicological Society and an honorary member of the Austrian Musicological Society.

In Bloomington, Hans belonged to Congregation Beth Shalom and was a founding member of the Bloomington Chamber Music Society. Bloomington Mayor Mark Kruzan honored Hans's life and career by declaring his ninety-third birthday (January 18, 2008) Hans Tischler Day. Hans passed away on November 18, 2010, in Bloomington.

Alice B. Tischler was born in Seattle, Washington, in 1935. She attended Roosevelt University, graduating with bachelor's (1958) and master's degrees in music (1960). In 1971, she earned her Master of Library Science degree from Indiana University. Alice worked as a music director for churches and synagogues, playing the organ, piano, and harpsichord. Prior to her time in Bloomington, she also served as a music literature instructor at the University of Chicago.

At IU, she served as a music cataloguer, Research Director of the Black Music Center, and an academic advisor. Her scholarly achievements include A Descriptive Bibliography of Art Music by Israeli Composers (1988, rev. 2011); Fifteen Black American Composers: A Bibliography of Their Works (1981); Karel Boleslav Jirák: A Catalog of His Works (1975); research on historical performance and analysis of the organ fugues of Johann Sebastian Bach; and a recent book on wine in opera. Active in politics, Alice ran the Monroe County Democratic headquarters for many elections, served as treasurer of the Democratic Women's Club, and served as precinct committeewoman for Perry 3 for several years.
Information retrieved from the information page for the Hans and Alice B. Tischler Scholarship/Fellowship.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This collection is arranged into the following series: I. PRINT MUSIC, II. SOUND RECORDINGS, III. PERSONAL EFFECTS

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The PRINT MUSIC series consists of printed and photocopy manuscript sheet music written by Israeli and Jewish composers that were collected by Alice and Hans Tischler. The SOUND RECORDINGS series consists of CD and DVD recordings of works written by Israeli and Jewish composers that were collected by Alice and Hans Tischler. The PERSONAL EFFECTS series consists of personal effects of Alice and Hans Tischler that were included with the collection such as research notes, gifted preprint articles, and photographs.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Preliminary inventorying and processing completed by Alice Tischler, date unknown. Rehousing completed by Indiana University Preservation Department, date unknown. Processing of sound recordings completed by Tarren Sexton in 2023. Processing of print music and personal effects completed by Sarah Helen Carter in 2023-2024.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: [Item], Alice and Hans Tischler Collection of Israeli and Jewish Composers, Collection MC122, Cook Music Library, Indiana University Bloomington, Indiana.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Tischler, Alice. A Descriptive Bibliography of Art Music by Israeli Composers. Harmonie Park Press, 2011. https://iucat.iu.edu/catalog/9627259.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Contains 6 works, bound together into a single volume. All works are "after Ibrahim Jabra's Poem 'I'm sick of you'". The poem is reproduced in the frontmatter of each individual work's score.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included works are: Hexaptychon I for mixed choir a capella, op. 36 (1996-97) [IMI 7175/I, 10 p.]; Hexaptychon II for contralto, oboe, and continuo, op. 37 (1996-97) [IMI 7175/II, 11 p.]; Hexaptychon III for string quartet, op. 38 (1996-97) [IMI 7175/III, 11 p.]; Hexaptychon IV for harp and string quartet, op. 39 (1996-97) [IMI 7175/IV, 14 p.]; Hexaptychon V for harp, op. 40 (1996-97) [IMI 7175/V, 8 p.]; Hexaptychon VI for piano 4 hands, op. 41 (1996-97) [IMI 7175/VI, 14 p.]

scopecontent.head: Part Details

did.unittitle: 7x7 bars for orchestra instruments and players

physdescstructured.dimensions: 61 cm (orchestral score), 42 cm (woodwind, brass, piano, and string sectional scores), 33 cm (trombone part and instruction page), 30 cm (flute part)

did.physdesc: 1 orchestral score + 4 sectional scores + 2 parts + 1 instruction page

odd.head: Part Details
odd.p: 1 orchestral score ("7x7 bars for orchestra instruments and players") + 1 woodwind section score ("7x7 bars for w.w and w.w players) + 1 brass section score ("7x7 bars for brass and brass players") + 1 percussion section score ("7x7 bars for perc. and perc. player") + 1 piano section score ("7x7 bars for piano and pianist") + 1 string section score ("7x7 bars for strings and string players") + 1 trombone part ("7x7 bars for trombone and trb player") + 1 flute part ("flauto part") + 1 instruction page ("Instruction page for 7x7")
Trombone part is 2 pages. All other parts are 1 oversized page of varying dimensions.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopy of instruction page; manuscript score for orchestra; sectional scores for woodwinds, brass, percussion, piano, and strings; and individual parts for trombone and flute

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Historical note on title page reads: "The original version of this String Quartet by Israel's prominent woman composer... won the Béla Bartok prize in Budapest 1949. It was revised by the composer in 1971 and was awarded the Acum Prize 1971 fo the sponsoring of the publication of works. This is the first publication of this work presenting the 1971 revisions by the composer."

did.unittitle: 6 works / Yossi Peles

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Three variations (out of five) for orchestra : 1997 (IBA Symphony Orchestra ; David Epstein, conductor) -- Fantasia on five chords : for horn, trumpet, trombone and tuba : 1976 (members of the Albany Quintet) -- Variations II for piano, 1977 (Bart Berman) -- Fantasia on twelve pitches : for 12 players, 1980 (Ensemble Musica Nova ; Ze'ev Dorman, conductor) -- Introversion : music for piano, 1990 (Yossi Peles) -- In memory of Emil Grünzweig : five short songs for contralto and four instruments, 1983 (Bracha Kol, contralto ; Adi Zektzer, clarinet ; Orli Glauber-Yidan, bass-clarinet ; Maria-Isabella Ordenung, violin ; Shaul Schwartz, violoncello)

did.unittitle: Alpha and omega

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: V. 1. Alpha and Omega -- V. 2. Alpha and Omega (conclusion). Preludes, book 1 / Debussy (arr. Shohat). Four arias from "Alpha and Omega" (voice and piano) -- V. 3. Israeli symphony (symphony no.1). Concertino for violin and orchestra -- V. 4. Vocalisa on ancient lamentations. The water's surface. bright winter. did.unittitle: Works by Ella Milch-Sheriff

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Disc 1. I'd like to enter the Book of Genesis -- Kitti and other women -- Woman in pass [that is, Woman in paths] -- Ze hayofi -- Ro'im otcha. Disc 2. A crown they will give unto you -- Goodnight, sweet ladies -- Little woman -- A China ballade -- Poem -- Duo for flute and cello. Disc 3. Can heaven be void : (English version) -- Can heaven be void (Hebrew version) -- Woman in pass [that is, Woman in paths]. Disc 4. Songs from the edge -- Variations on an Israeli song -- 4 miniatures for 2 violas -- O Lord, God of Hosts.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The two materials in this sub-series were found housed together in an unlabeled folder. While the items' exact relationship is unclear, it has been decided to keep the items together in deference to that potentially original order.
